summary: A brief description of a 1788 letter from Immanuel Kant to Karl Leonhard Reinhold. In the letter, Kant expresses a desire to start a journal dedicated to the systematic examination of speculative and practical philosophy, while noting his burden of administrative duties.

principles.statement: Controversial philosophical points must be examined thoroughly and systematically.
principles.rationale: Kant proposed starting a journal with Reinhold and others specifically dedicated to the rigorous examination of speculative and practical philosophy.
principles.source_quote: thoroughly and systematically the most controversial points of the whole of speculative and practical philosophy.

signature_quotes.text: thoroughly and systematically the most controversial points of the whole of speculative and practical philosophy.
signature_quotes.context: Describing the intended focus of a new philosophical journal he hoped to start with Reinhold.
